turns.text: Q2 fiscal 2026 was exceptional. Cloud revenue was $9.1 billion, up 29% in constant currency. OCI revenue grew 59%. We are raising our fiscal 2026 cloud revenue guidance to $50 billion from $48 billion, in line with what we indicated at last quarter's call. RPO grew to $156 billion. We expect cloud revenue to exceed $25 billion in the second half of fiscal 2026 alone, implying our run rate will exit fiscal 2026 above $50 billion annualized.

We are winning the AI infrastructure race because of our cluster networking technology and our ability to deliver operational AI agents at enterprise scale. Our agents platform crossed 2,000 production enterprise customers this quarter. We expect to announce a major expansion of our strategic partnership with Microsoft in January 2026, which will make OCI the preferred AI infrastructure for Microsoft's enterprise customers in regulated industries.
Our next question comes from Brent Thill at Jefferies.
Larry, 2,000 production agent customers — can you help frame average contract value and whether these are net new OCI workloads or migrations of existing database customers to agent workflows?

statements.claim: OCI revenue grew 59% in Q2 fiscal 2026.
Oracle is raising fiscal 2026 cloud revenue guidance to $50 billion.
Oracle agents platform crossed 2,000 production enterprise customers.
Oracle RPO grew to $156 billion in Q2 fiscal 2026.
Oracle will announce a major expansion of its strategic partnership with Microsoft in January 2026.
